title.text: The link I have communicated to users for participating doesn't work

body:0001.text: Why is the link I shared not working?

body:0002.text: If users receive an error message saying they don’t have permission to view your project or survey, it’s usually due to one of the following reasons:

body:0004.text: If your project hasn’t been published yet, it won’t be visible to users (unless they are using the preview link)

body:0005.text: ℹ️ Action: Publish the project before sharing the link.

body:0007.text: Go to the General tab to your project settings → Access Rights and check the Visibility settings.

body:0008.text: If visibility is limited to specific users or groups, anyone outside of that selection won’t be able to access the project.

body:0009.text: ℹ️ Action: Adjust the visibility settings to include your intended participants.

body:0010.text: 3. The wrong link is being shared

body:0011.text: Make sure you’re sharing the project URL, not the survey URL.

body:0012.text: If you share the survey URL directly and it requires login, email verification, or demographic pre-screening, users may get an error.

body:0013.text: ℹ️ Action: Always share the project URL, which ensures users complete any required steps before accessing the survey.
